observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive, through the Head of the General Services Office, undertake disposal of unserviceable properties to prevent further deterioration and decline in their value and to generate additional income from their sale.

We recommended that the City formulate and pass GAD Code or ordinances  and establish and maintain a GAD Database to ensure and sustain the LGUs critical consciousness in supporting gender and development, women’s empowerment and responding to gender issues as prescribed in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01. 

We further recommended that the reviewed and approved  Annual GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) by the DILG Regional Office and the corresponding accomplishment report be prepared and submitted to COA Audit Team pursuant to COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.

We recommended and Management agreed that the (i) City Accountant  and CGS Officer maintain complete and updated PPELC and PC, respectively for each class of property to record the acquisition, adjustments and disposal thereof; (ii) Inventory Committee exert extra effort to conduct complete physical count of all property, plant, and equipment of the City Government by type on annual basis and submit to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year pursuant to existing rules and regulations; (iii) City Accountant and City Engineer coordinate with each other to trace the necessary records/documents pertaining to those projects that were still accounted under Construction in Progress for proper recording in the books for a fair presentation of PPE accounts in the financial statements; and (iv) City Accountant, City Assessor and Heads of the General Services Office (GSO) and City Planning and Development Office (CPDO) (a) coordinate with each other and reconcile their records to ascertain the correctness of the Land account balance; (b) register in the name of City Government the land which were not yet covered by TCT as proof of ownership of the asset; and (c) the Accounting Office record in the books of accounts all unrecorded parcels of land acquired by the City and maintain Real Property Ledger Card for each land property to contain the necessary information relative thereto.

We recommended and Management agreed that the City Accountant (i) follow strictly the provisions of Section 89 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances for a more efficient and effective internal control system; (ii) ensure that all cash advances are reported on as soon as the purpose for which it was granted has been served and that no cash advances remained unliquidated at the end of the year; (iii) continue sending demand letters to the employees and to those who have already transferred to another government agency and who were resigned/retired from the service; and  (iv) the City Legal Officer, if warranted, apply all possible legal remedies for the settlement of cash advances granted to officials and employees.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive, through the Head of the General Services Office undertake disposal of unserviceable motor vehicles/heavy equipment to prevent further deterioration and decline in their value and to generate additional income from their sale. We further recommended that the necessary procedures and documents on the disposal of unserviceable properties be undertaken and prepared to ensure their proper disposal.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the City Economic Enterprise and Public Utilities Management Officer and the City Treasurer to: (i) issue follow-up communications and/ or demand letters to stall occupants for the settlement of their respective delinquent accounts on market rentals; (ii) initiate legal action, if warranted, to collect the long overdue   arrearages; (iii) make a thorough study on the appropriateness of the rental rates being charged by  the City as compared with the current prevailing rental rates; (iv) justify and support recommendation for the enactment of appropriate Market Code or any ordinance by the Sanggunian for the market rental rate adjustment; and  (vi) effect the execution and/or renewal of lease contract/agreement, if necessary, between the City Government and the stall occupants.

We recommended and Management agreed that the City General Service Office and City Engineering Office accomplish properly the POWs, PO and IARs and to indicate therein all the data required for the implementation of various infrastructure projects to determine any delay on deliveries and in the project implementation either by contract or by administration so that the applicable liquidated damages could be imposed against contractors/suppliers.

We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive require the City General Services Officer to submit all perfected contracts/purchase orders/notice of deliveries and their supporting documents to COA within the prescribed period pursuant to the above- cited regulations.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (i) instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to justify why the City resorted to negotiated procurement under small value procurement instead of public bidding having a total cost of ₱1,388,200.00 which exceeded its thresholds if procurements of same items in about same period were consolidated; and (ii) direct the officials of CALMO who were in-charged in the conduct of the skills training program to explain the deficiencies noted in the particular attendance sheets of participants for every training conducted.

We recommended and Management agreed that the City Accountant and the CSWDO (i) strictly adhere to the fundamental principles in the disbursement of funds as prescribed under Section 4(6) of PD  No. 1445; and (ii) correct deficiencies and submit the above-mentioned lacking documents to the Audit Team for further evaluation/verification by the audit team.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the City Engineering Office and the BUB Focal person of the respective implementing offices to strictly monitor the implementation of projects as targeted and planned and to ensure that the funds are utilized for its intended purpose so that the immediate desired benefits from such projects could be attained by the City’s beneficiaries/constituents.

We reiterated our recommendations and Management agreed that the City Solid Waste Management Board (CSWMB) and the Environmental Protection Division (EPD) (i) exert effort to expedite the establishment and completion of the Integrated Solid Waste Management Facility; (ii) involve closely the Barangay officials in the strict implementation on the existing environmental laws and intensify the information campaign on the “no segregation, no collection policy and the “no-littering policy” among the residents; and (iii) exercise strong political will to implement the environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment as mandated under Republic Act  No. 9003.

We recommended and Management agreed that the:
Local Chief Executive (i) direct the concerned City officials and traffic management personnel under the PNP’s supervision, to fully enforce the existing City ordinance in prohibiting/banning the plying of tricycles along the national highway of the City, particularly on inner lanes, to prevent any hazardous incidence thru road accidents, which may pose imminent danger both to pedestrians and motorists’ lives and properties and to ease the traffic congestion which resulted from the plying of many tricycles in the national highways; (ii) take into consideration the installation of an adequate and efficient CCTVs along the national highways, including the nearby barangays of the City as an effective monitoring tool to assess/evaluate traffic management and capture data on any occurrence of criminal acts and in providing immediate assistance to the public in case of road accidents/criminalities; and (iii) direct the City Human Resource Officer and the City General Service Officer to consider the necessity of hiring additional traffic personnel to man the critical or traffic/accident-prone areas and provide the necessary communication equipment and facilities to monitor incidents that require immediate action/rescue by the City Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office; and
Members of the SP revisit their existing ordinances to make necessary amendments or enact appropriate ordinance on the regulation for a more effective operation of tricycles within the City by taking into consideration the total prohibition/banning of tricycles to operate/ply their routes on the City national highways, provided that there are identified alternative routes, pursuant to the directives and guidelines of the DILG and DOTC for public safety and order in both pedestrians and motorists.

We recommended and Management agreed that the members of the City Local School Board (i) request for inputs from the DepEd Officials/School Division Superintendent in the prioritization of SEF projects and expenditures in accordance with the DepEd criteria and with due regard to the provisions of laws, rules and regulations prescribing the limitations on the use of the fund; and (ii) ensure that the budgeted amount for each year on SEF projects is implemented/utilized for the efficient and timely delivery of targeted services and benefits to the constituents in accordance with the proper utilization of the fund.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office to (i) ensure that executed programs/projects and activities charged to LDRRMF were in line with the LDRRMP; (ii) monitor the programmed plans and activities in the LDRRMP for the maximum utilization of the LDRRMF to its fullest intent; and (iii) formulate programs and activities strictly in accordance with COA Circular 2012-002 and Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 for proper allocation and utilization of LDRRMF.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office to refrain from using DRRMF for programs/projects/activities which are not directly related with disaster preparedness, mitigation, response and rescue operations and strictly adhere to the provisions of existing rules and regulations on the proper utilization of LDRRM Funds.

We recommended that the LDRRMO (i) refrain from using LDRRMF for programs/projects/activities which are not directly related with disaster preparedness, mitigation, and response and rescue operations and follow strictly the rules on utilization of LDRRMF as stated in COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 and (ii) submit justification for the utilization of cash donation for the purchase of resettlement site for displaced families in the construction of Central Luzon Expressway (CLEX).

We reiterated our recommendations and Management agreed that the City Government,  through the City Development Council (i) ensure that the mandatory 20% Development Fund appropriated out of the Internal Revenue Allotment be optimally utilized to implement prioritized identified programs, projects and activities to help achieve the desirable outcomes on socio-economic development and environmental management pursuant to the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1; and (ii) come up with a good planning in identifying priority programs and projects with its corresponding full implementation for the immediate benefits that could be derived therefrom.

We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed that the City formulate and pass GAD Code or ordinances and establish/maintain a GAD Database to ensure and sustain the LGUs critical consciousness in supporting gender and development, women’s empowerment and responding to gender issues as prescribed in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01. We further recommended that the reviewed and approved  Annual GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) by the DILG Regional Office and the corresponding accomplishment report be prepared and submitted to COA Audit Team pursuant to COA Circular No, 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (i) City Budget Officer to include in the appropriation sufficient amount for the insurance coverage of all insurable properties of the City; and  (ii) City General Services Officer to insure all insurable properties of the City with the GSIS and to coordinate with the City Accountant and the City Treasurer  for the timely processing and payment of GSIS insurance in compliance with the provisions of RA No. 656.

We recommended and Management agreed that the City Accountant (i) continue to coordinate with the City Treasurer on the prompt remittance of all GSIS premiums pursuant to the provisions of RA No. 8291 so that the employees can enjoy all the privileges of a GSIS member; and (ii) exert more effort in identifying the nature of the previous years’ balances of ₱164,502.88 and ₱40,074.44 due to GSIS under SEF and Trust Funds, respectively and make the necessary adjustment thereof.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the Accounting and Treasury Offices to coordinate with each other for the prompt transmission of disbursement vouchers so that timely recording of transactions could be made by the Accounting Office and that the prompt remittances of taxes to the BIR could likewise be processed. We further recommended that the Management continue to strictly adhere with the existing revenue rules and regulations on the withholding of taxes and prompt remittances thereof to the BIR.

We recommended and Management agreed to exercise due prudence in the disbursement of government funds to avoid audit suspensions/disallowances. We further recommended for the total settlement of PEI overpayment and submission of documents for the unsettled audit suspension of ₱5,574,600.00.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Local Chief Executive require the Accountant to

(a) promptly adjust the reconciling items reflected in the BRS to present a more accurate cash in bank account balances and other affected accounts;

(b) work on the submission of the unrecorded disbursement vouchers; and 

(c) exert more efforts in finding alternative ways to prove validity of the debit/credit memos.
observations.managementAction:
The amount of ₱408,200.32  for purchase of spare parts of heavy equipment was deducted from the accountable officer’s terminal leave; 
Purchase of various sutures amounting to ₱460,749.21 was reconstructed and recorded.

City officials with outstanding cash advances were no longer given additional cash advances unless the previous cash advances were fully liquidated.

Several old cash advances were liquidated



Demand Letters were periodically sent.



The City Accountant forwarded the latest monitoring report for cash advance to the City Legal Office.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations that the Local Chief Executive require the 

(a) City Accountant to trace the composition and nature of the CIP account, effect the necessary adjustments, and prepare the corresponding subsidiary ledger for each project to monitor the completion of each project; and 



(b) concerned offices to facilitate the necessary documentation of the completed projects and submit the same to the City Accountant for proper recording.
observations.managementAction: Prepared subsidiary ledgers for each project for proper monitoring of CIP account balances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Assessor 

(a) conduct regular survey of idle lands to generate an updated list with the corresponding assessed values thereof to be submitted to the City Treasurer for collection; and 

(b) maintain and update the list of idle lands on a regular basis.

observations.managementAction: Recorded the honoraria for consultants under the Consultancy Services account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that the City Accountant and the City Treasurer 

(a) strictly comply with the regulation on the timely rendition of accounts and financial reports in order not to delay in the verification and post-audit activities; and 



(b) observe the due dates of submission of the required financial reports and documents to the Audit Team.

We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed that the City Mayor require the General Services Office to submit all perfected contracts/purchase orders and their supporting documents to the Audit Team within the prescribed period pursuant to the above cited regulations.

We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive require the General Services Officer and the City Engineer to strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 on the information and publicity on the PPAs of the Government Agencies to promote good governance through transparency and accountability to the people and to enable the Audit Team to comply with the submission of the Quarterly Report of the PPAs to the Regional Director for monitoring compliance.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the City Accountant and City Treasurer to strictly comply on the proper accounting and reporting guidelines on the receipt and utilization of LDRRMF pursuant to COA Circulars No. 2012 – 002 and 2014 – 002 dated September 12, 2012 and April 15, 2014, respectively.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant maintained a different utilization report for cash donations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management stop providing insurance to Disaster Risk Reduction and Management personnel, instead, provide insurance and necessary benefits to ACDVs only (if any), to encourage volunteers to support disaster risk reduction and management activities of the LGU.

We recommended that the members of the City School Board 

(a) prepare a balanced budget with conscious and concerted efforts of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization; 

(b) request for inputs from the DepEd Official/School Division Superintendent in the prioritization of SEF projects and expenditures in accordance with the DepEd criteria and with due regard to the provisions of laws, rules and regulations prescribing the limitations on the use of the fund; and

(c) maximize the utilization of SEF to attain its objectives and take appropriate actions such as realignment or reprograming, if necessary.

We recommended that the City School Board 

(a) exercise due prudence in the proper utilization of Special Education Fund (SEF) pursuant to the rules and regulations on SEF utilization; and 

(b) explain/cite legal basis in using such funds for allowances of non-teaching personnel instead for research expenses, otherwise, this would be suspended in audit. We further recommended to submit copy of SEF Supplemental Budgets with corresponding Resolutions duly approved by the Local School Board pertaining thereto.
observations.managementAction: Stopped payment of research subsidy.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the City Government return to the Department of Finance (DOF)-Bureau of Treasury the unutilized PDAF which has been declared unconstitutional by the Supreme Court and was not utilized within the time the Notice of Cash Allocation was issued. Moreover, this is in compliance with one of the audit instructions embodied under unnumbered COA Memorandum dated March 26, 2014 directing the Audit Team to ensure that the said unutilized PDAF be returned to the DOF.
observations.managementAction: Unexpended balance of PDAF was returned to Department of Finance (DOF)-Bureau of Treasury.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that the City Development Council, the Sanggunian, the City Budget Officer and the City Accountant 

(a) refrain from appropriating/allocating funds for programs/projects/activities (PPAs) under the 20% Development Fund which are not capital in nature; 

(b) ensure that the fund is only utilized for its intended purpose; and 

(c) stop the granting of honoraria to Project Management Unit which is contrary to the said DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular.
observations.managementAction: Stopped payment of honoraria to the Project Monitoring Unit (PMU).

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Government, through the City Development Council 

(a) ensure that the mandatory 20% Development Fund appropriated out of the Internal Revenue Allotment be optimally utilized to implement prioritized identified programs, projects and activities to help achieve the desirable outcomes on socio-economic development and environmental management pursuant to the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1; and 

(b) come up with a better method in identifying priority programs and projects with its corresponding full implementation for the immediate benefits that could be derived therefrom.

We recommended and Management agreed that the City Accountant 

(a) continue to coordinate with the City Treasurer on the prompt remittance of all GSIS premiums pursuant to the provisions of RA No. 8291 so that the employees can enjoy all the privileges of a GSIS member; 

(b) make a follow-up from the GSIS the guidelines on the hazard premium contributions for appropriate action; and 

(c) exert more effort in identifying the nature of the previous years’ balances of ₱174,402.88 and ₱40,075.03 due to GSIS under the SEF and Trust Fund, respectively and make the necessary adjustment thereof.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive require the City Treasurer to submit promptly the corresponding disbursement vouchers to the Accounting Office for the latter’s processing of the same for the prompt remittance of taxes to the BIR.
observations.managementAction: The City Treasurer was informed on the prompt submission of the concerned disbursement vouchers

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to exercise due prudence in the disbursement of government funds to avoid audit suspensions/disallowances.
